purpose: Adds an early check in hci_cmd_sync_queue for HCI_UNREGISTER and returns -ENODEV so commands are not queued after hci_unregister_dev, preventing use-after-free after the timeout.
Remove an unnecessary #include <asm/termios.h> from drivers/tty/n_hdlc.c because linux/tty.h already pulls linux/termios.h; this simplifies and unifies header usage.
Update perf-c2c documentation to include the new 'N/A' store metric (store accesses where memory level is not available) and adjust report table descriptions to reflect this addition.

Fixes hugetlb vma-lock handling: ensure new vma gets its own vma_lock when vm_area_dup copies the pointer, acquire/free the vma lock correctly during unmap to avoid races, and make pmd-sharing require a valid vma_lock (vm_private_data).
Adds AVDD-supply and MICVDD-supply properties to the realtek,rt5682s devicetree binding, marks them required and updates the example to show how to specify the regulators.
Adds a missing iounmap() call in zorro7xx_remove_one(), guarded by a base-address check, to undo an ioremap() performed during probe and prevent leaked I/O mappings on device removal.
Removes an unused struct member (work) from mlx5e_async_ctx in ktls_tx.c; a small internal cleanup with no functional or API change.
Change cfg80211_auth_request.key_idx from u8 to signed s8 so it can hold -1 (unused) and valid 0..3 indexes, avoiding static-checker warnings and potential unsigned-wrap misbehavior.
Sorts CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS lines in two RISC-V defconfigs to match savedefconfig ordering; purely a config-ordering/formatting change with no behavior impact.

Adds a small helper mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() that encapsulates the CAN-FD / listen-only check and replaces duplicated bitmask tests across FIFO and ring initialization, improving clarity and centralizing logic.

Update rtc selftests to detect when /dev/rtc is absent and SKIP tests instead of asserting; adds checks for self->fd == -1 && errno == ENOENT and calls SKIP in multiple test cases to avoid spurious failures on systems without RTC hardware.
Removes a redundant assignment to 'bytes' in snd_echo_midi_output_write, eliminating a dead-store/clang warning. Change is stylistic and does not alter behavior.

Decouples rcuscale from TASKS_RCU so rcuscale can be built without RCU Tasks: wraps TASKS_OPS in #ifdef CONFIG_TASKS_RCU, adjusts Kconfig and rcutorture configs, reducing unnecessary TASKS_RCU builds and related tracing complexity.
Typo fix in a comment inside arch/x86/kvm/mmu/spte.h: removes duplicated word 'be split' to read correctly. Purely a comment/documentation change with no functional impact.

Reverts prior change that populated the DCN315 clock table from DCFCLK data; restores safer clock-selection logic (find_clk_for_voltage, scanning DfPstateTable) and adds checks to avoid a no-read underflow when pmfw disables dcfclk states.
Add renesas,r9a07g054-mali compatible to the Mali Bifrost devicetree binding YAML so the RZ/V2L (R9A07G054) SoC (Mali‑G31) is documented and accepted by DT validators.

Rename struct pcie_port to dw_pcie_rp across the DesignWare PCIe host driver and headers, updating function signatures, local variables and usages to use the 'dw_' prefixed name for consistency and clearer namespacing.
Adjusts the printk format for eth->h_proto from %#06hx to %#06x so the format matches the promoted type of ntohs(eth->h_proto); a small, local change to address a Clang -Wformat warning.

Adjusts the branch-range check in arch/mips/kernel/jump_label.c by casting BIT(25) to long so the comparison is signed, preventing negative offsets from incorrectly triggering WARN_ON and avoiding signed/unsigned comparison issues.

Eliminates the HW_VAR_H2C_FW_P2P_PS_OFFLOAD switch case and calls rtl8188e_set_p2p_ps_offload_cmd() directly; removes the corresponding enum. This simplifies the HAL indirection and calls the function where needed.
Makes a local symbol static to silence a sparse warning by restricting its linkage; changes cavium_erratum_23154_cpus from global to file-local static const.

Edits devicetree binding examples for MDSS to remove an unnecessary/incorrect interrupt flags cell (e.g. <0 IRQ_TYPE_LEVEL_HIGH> -> <0>), reflecting that the MDSS interrupt provider uses a single-cell interrupt spec.

Generalizes allocation and handling of control arrays: allocates space for arrays in struct v4l2_ctrl_ref, renames dynamic-array fields to generic array names, and updates allocation/validation logic to prepare for drivers resizing arrays.

Adds several Qualcomm SoC IDs and PMIC model strings to drivers/soc/qcom/socinfo.c so the socinfo driver recognizes SM8350, SC8280XP, SA8540P, another SM8450 entry and multiple PMICs found on those boards.

Replace explicit switch that printed 'A'..'E' or "unknown" with logic that maps chip_cut 0..15 to letters 'A'..'P' and prints a single char, ensuring all chip cuts are recognised and simplifying the code.
Removes a redundant software '.is_enabled' field and its helper functions from ux500 PRCMU clock driver because the core clock framework already maintains enable state; cleans up unused code paths.

Reorganizes arch/riscv/mm/init.c to group and move XIP-specific #ifdefs, extern declarations, and the pt_ops XIP_FIXUP macro so XIP-only code is adjacent; this is a preprocessor/layout cleanup with no behavioral change.
Sets device-tree node statuses to "okay" for qupv3_id_0/1 and gpi_dma0/1 in the sm6350-lena DTS, enabling QUP and GPI DMA hardware so functioning I2C nodes can be added later.

Corrects and clarifies comments in drivers/thermal/rzg2l_thermal.c: updates TS_CODE_CAP_TIMES comment, fixes 'caliberation'->'calibration', and reformats block comments to meet coding style; no functional changes.
Replaces a busy-wait loop that could hang when failing to acquire the hardware semaphore with an early check that logs an error and returns, preventing infinite hang on surprise hardware removal.
Replaces old master/slave DAI/clocking terminology with modern provider/consumer terms in pcm3168a, updates masks, format checks and error messages to match current ASoC naming and semantics.
Remove an obsolete #ifndef IS_SWAPFILE fallback in fs/nfs/file.c that defined IS_SWAPFILE(inode) as 0; this is a simple cleanup because IS_SWAPFILE is always defined.

Adds GTP Echo Request/Response handling for GTPv0 and GTPv1u: implements validation and response builders, routing helper, restart-count attribute, new IE/packet structs, and hooks to auto-respond when kernel-created sockets exist.

Replaces cpumask_next_* usages with cpumask_first_* when the search effectively starts at -1 (start==0), and adds cpumask_first_zero(); updates several callers (blk-mq, virtio, qbman, clocksource) to use the new helper for small efficiency and clarity improvements.

Remove the code path that queried the firmware IOVAR 'dtim_assoc' (which firmware no longer supports) after roaming; the change avoids repeated error messages by not attempting this unsupported query and simplifies bss info handling.

Adds a new board config 'adl_rt5682' and an ACPI match entry to support the ALC5682 headset codec on platforms without a speaker amplifier, wiring the codec to SSP0 following Intel BT offload design and selecting appropriate topology/firmware files.
Adds a guard in io_sendmsg_zc to check SOCK_SUPPORT_ZC and return -EOPNOTSUPP when the socket doesn't support zerocopy, aligning sendmsg behavior with existing zerocopy send request handling.

Update kerneldoc-preamble.sty to test for xeCJK.sty before loading and gate CJK enablement on both the Noto Sans CJK font and the xeCJK package, avoiding xelatex failures on systems with texlive-xecjk absent.
Updates scripts/checkpatch.pl to ignore lines beginning with 'case' for the macro-complexity check, preventing false positives for 'case' macros by adding the regex exemption /^case\b/.

Replace the simple-KMS helpers with standard DRM atomic helpers in simpledrm: convert plane/crtc/connector/encoder setup, use drm_atomic checks and helper functions, reorganize update/disable paths; stated to be no functional change.

Adds a jpeg decode worker and multicore decode support: HW allocation/put helpers, worker that selects/free HW, waitqueue, runtime/clk handling, and new m2m ops and device-tree driver data to run multiple decode HWs in parallel.
Removes many VPRINTK() verbose printk calls and the ata_scsi_dump_cdb() helper and its prototype across libata, relying on tracepoints instead to reduce redundant debug output and clean up code.

Implements support to use extra vblank (extended blank during FREESYNC) to enter zstate: adds flags, stream adjust fields, optimized_min_dst_y_next_start calculations, hubp program_extended_blank, and integrates checks into DML/hwseq and clk decisions.
Changes the hqd_destroy() callback prototype: resets the third parameter from uint32_t to enum kfd_preempt_type to match implementations and avoid a Clang kCFI indirect-call type mismatch at runtime.

Fixes the stm32 pinctrl device-tree binding schema by renaming the property from 'reset' to the correct 'resets' and adding 'additionalProperties: false' to the gpio pattern, tightening validation and correcting the documented binding.
Adds a conditional per-file CFLAGS entry to ath9k Makefile that disables -Warray-bounds when KBUILD_EXTRA_WARN is not set, silencing a spurious GCC 12 warning as a temporary workaround.

Replaces pm_runtime_get_sync() with pm_runtime_resume_and_get() in sun8i-ss PRNG to simplify runtime PM handling and error path; small API change expected to keep behavior but improve correctness/clarity.

Refactors io_uring fixed-file handling by moving fixed file installation/removal into filetable.c, adding __io_fixed_fd_install and io_fixed_fd_remove that take an io_ring_ctx (not io_kiocb), updating headers and call sites to use the ctx-based API.

Updates the imx8mq VPU devicetree binding to split the previously combined VPU node into separate G1 and G2 nodes: introduces new compatible strings (nxp,imx8mq-vpu-g1/g2), reduces reg/interrupts/clocks to single entries, updates examples and power-domain usage, and deprecates the old combined compatible.

Inline the previously separate IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON macro into IWL_DEVICE_BZ in the iwlwifi 22000 configuration, removing an extra indirection and simplifying the macro definitions in cfg/22000.c; no functional change.
